title: T-010 — cost_per_kg dual-ownership RLS policy (D-FIN-9, §13.2)
prompt:
# T-010 — cost_per_kg dual-ownership RLS policy

PRD: docs/prd/10-FINANCE-PRD.md §13.2 D-FIN-9 dual ownership; §13.1 standard_cost.approved event hook; §6.1 row 6 standard_costs.item_id FK→items.
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Add a column-level RLS policy `cost_per_kg_write_finance_only` on `items.cost_per_kg` that:
- Allows READ to all RLS-permitted readers (per existing items RLS).
- Allows UPDATE on `cost_per_kg` ONLY when the calling session role bears the `fin.standard_cost.approve` permission (signaled via SESSION GUC `app.permissions` populated by `withOrgContext`) OR is `app.current_role()='finance_worker'` (used by background worker writing on approval).
Other columns of `items` retain their existing 03-TECHNICAL write owners. This is the technical realization of D-FIN-9: schema owner = 03-TECH, lifecycle owner = 10-FIN.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0054_items_cost_per_kg_policy.sql`:
   - Pre-flight check: `items.cost_per_kg NUMERIC(15,4)` column must exist (CHECK against `information_schema.columns`). If missing, raise migration error citing 03-TECHNICAL T-074 must be applied first.
   - Drop any pre-existing `items_write_*` policy that grants blanket UPDATE on cost_per_kg (audit prior 03-TECHNICAL policies first via `pg_policies`).
   - Create new policy `items_cost_per_kg_write` ON items FOR UPDATE TO public USING (org_id = app.current_org_id()) WITH CHECK (org_id = app.current_org_id() AND (current_setting('app.permissions', true) LIKE '%fin.standard_cost.approve%' OR current_setting('app.current_role', true) = 'finance_worker')). The policy fires only when the UPDATE statement touches `cost_per_kg`; use a column-level guard via `BEFORE UPDATE` trigger that compares OLD.cost_per_kg to NEW.cost_per_kg and rejects when changed by non-finance session.
   - Trigger function `enforce_cost_per_kg_finance_write()` BEFORE UPDATE on items: IF NEW.cost_per_kg IS DISTINCT FROM OLD.cost_per_kg AND NOT (current_setting('app.permissions',true) LIKE '%fin.standard_cost.approve%' OR current_setting('app.current_role',true) = 'finance_worker') THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'cost_per_kg can only be updated by finance lifecycle owner (D-FIN-9)'.
2. Update `withOrgContext` HOF (Foundation T-125) to set `app.permissions` GUC containing the comma-joined list of caller's permissions (one-time set per request). [If T-125 doesn't yet expose permissions, document the contract change here and add a migration follow-up — but do NOT touch packages/db/src/orgContext.ts beyond reading; keep it minimal.]

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0054_items_cost_per_kg_policy.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/cost-per-kg-policy.test.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/permissions-context.ts [create] (helper to set app.permissions GUC)

## Acceptance criteria (Given-When-Then)
- Given session with app.permissions including 'fin.standard_cost.approve', when UPDATE items SET cost_per_kg=X runs, then it succeeds.
- Given session WITHOUT fin permission and not finance_worker role, when UPDATE items SET cost_per_kg=X runs, then it fails citing D-FIN-9.
- Given session WITHOUT fin permissions updates any OTHER items column, when UPDATE runs, then it succeeds.
- Given background worker sets app.current_role='finance_worker', when UPDATE items SET cost_per_kg=X runs, then it succeeds. AND given pg_policies on items is inspected, when listed, then items_cost_per_kg_write policy exists referencing the finance_worker role or fin permission.
_(AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F13 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost))_

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: cost-per-kg-policy.test.ts seeds 2 sessions (finance-mgr vs ops-mgr) + 1 worker; asserts each AC scenario.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test cost-per-kg-policy`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not block 03-TECHNICAL writes to OTHER item columns — only cost_per_kg is finance-locked.
- Do not use `current_user`/`session_user` for the permission check — those are Postgres role names, not application permission strings. Use the `app.permissions` GUC.
- Do not skip the migration pre-flight check for `items.cost_per_kg` column existence.
- Do not introduce circular dependency with packages/rbac (DB layer reads GUC only; rbac sets it).
- Do not weaken existing items RLS for other columns.

pipeline_inputs.description: Column-level write policy: cost_per_kg writable only by finance permission/role; other items columns unchanged.
pipeline_inputs.details: Implements D-FIN-9 dual ownership: 03-TECHNICAL owns items schema (DDL); 10-FIN owns cost_per_kg lifecycle (writes on approval). Realized via BEFORE UPDATE trigger that compares OLD/NEW values and gates on `app.permissions` GUC set by withOrgContext (Foundation T-125 contract extension documented here). Includes pre-flight check for items.cost_per_kg column existence. [AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F13 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost)]
